question.text: A person indulged in fraudulent and unfair trade practices and made profits of Rs. 6 crore. Under Section 15HA of the SEBI Act, what is the maximum penalty that can be imposed?

answer.text: Rs. 25 crore (since it is higher than three times the profits of Rs. 18 crore)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Section 15HA, the penalty for fraudulent and unfair trade practices can extend to twenty-five crore rupees or three times the amount of profits made, whichever is higher. Three times Rs. 6 crore = Rs. 18 crore. Since Rs. 25 crore > Rs. 18 crore, the maximum penalty is Rs. 25 crore. **The Trap:** Rs. 18 crore (three times the profit) seems correct but the provision says 'whichever is higher', and Rs. 25 crore is higher in this case. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A and Series IX also address fraudulent trade practices and associated penalties. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 'whichever is higher' clause ensures that even when profits are relatively small, the penalty can still be substantial at Rs. 25 crore.
